text: Add src/client/ApiEaseUpdateRequestClient.js as the canonical Node client for updating a single request by id.
goal: Implement apiease read, apiease update, and apiease delete as public CLI commands.
context: The public CRUD exercise already uses PUT api/v1/resources/requests/:requestId for request updates.
Request update payloads should stay aligned with the existing request contract validation used for create.
steps: Implement an updateRequest method that accepts apiBaseUrl, apiKey, shopDomain, requestId, and request.
Reuse the existing request contract validator before making the API call so update payloads follow the same request shape expectations as create.
Call the versioned request item endpoint with PUT and normalize success and failure responses into the established CLI result shape.
constraints: Do not introduce a second CLI-specific request schema for updates.
Do not move file loading or argument parsing into the client.
acceptanceCriteria: The client issues PUT requests to api/v1/resources/requests/<encoded requestId> with the validated JSON request body and standard authentication headers.
Invalid request payloads fail locally with the same machine-readable validation structure already used for request contract failures.
Backend JSON responses, transport errors, and non-JSON failures are returned as structured results consumable by the CLI layer.
